headline: Anthropic / Time profile — AI safety messaging amid commercial push (May 22):
summary: A *Time* feature published today profiles Anthropic co-founder Jack Clark, examining the tension between the company's existential-risk warnings and its aggressive commercial Claude Code rollout.

headline: Google I/O 2026 wrap — Gemini 3.5 Flash GA, TPU 8 dual-chip, Antigravity (May 19–20):
summary: Google's annual developer conference at Shoreline Amphitheatre concluded this week.

headline: Google DeepMind acquihires Contextual AI researchers (May 19–20):
summary: DeepMind brought on 20+ researchers from enterprise RAG startup Contextual AI under a non-exclusive licensing arrangement valued at $80–90 million.

headline: Anthropic Code w/ Claude 2026 — London leg (May 19), platform announcements now live:
summary: Anthropic's developer conference hit London on 19 May (Tokyo follows June 10).

headline: Anthropic Claude for Small Business (May 13):
summary: Launched at no extra cost for existing Claude Team and Enterprise subscribers.

headline: Cohere Command A+ 05-2026 — 218B MoE, Apache 2.0, vision, native citations (May 20–21):
summary: Cohere's first fully open-weight frontier model and its first multimodal release.

headline: ByteDance Lance — 3B any-to-any multimodal, today's top HF trending model (released May 19):
summary: Lance is a unified multimodal model covering image/video understanding, generation, and editing in a single framework.

headline: Sapient Inc HRM-Text-1B — hierarchical reasoning base model (May 17):
summary: A 1.18B-parameter text-generation base model (pre-alignment, non-instruction-tuned) built on a novel `hrm_text` architecture implementing Hierarchical Reasoning Machines.

headline: Tencent Hy-MT2 multilingual translation MoE pair (May 11):
summary: Two open translation models from Tencent's Hunyuan team: `Hy-MT2-1.8B` (dense, HunyuanV1 architecture) and `Hy-MT2-30B-A3B` (sparse MoE, 30B total / 3B active, Hy-V3 architecture).

headline: Supertone supertonic-3 — on-device multilingual TTS (May 6, still trending #2 on HF):
summary: Supertonic-3 is an ONNX-format text-to-speech model running on-device, covering English, Korean, Japanese, Arabic, and 20+ additional languages.

headline: Unsloth Qwen3.6 GGUF ports (trending for local inference):
summary: Unsloth shipped imatrix-quantized GGUF variants of Qwen/Qwen3.6-27B (trending score 232, 532K downloads) and Qwen/Qwen3.6-35B-A3B (180, 466K downloads).

headline: LMArena leaderboard state — GPT-5.5 Pro leads SWE-bench, Opus 4.7 leads thinking mode (as of May 22):
summary: The Chatbot Arena platform (rebranded LMArena / Arena.ai since January 2026) shows GPT-5.5 Pro at 88.7% SWE-bench Verified, displacing Claude Opus 4.7 (87.6% in extended thinking mode) from the coding-benchmark top slot.

headline: Gemini 3.5 Flash benchmark claims — agentic tasks, 4× speed, $1.50/M input (GA'd May 20):
summary: Google's vendor-published numbers show Gemini 3.5 Flash outperforming Gemini 3.1 Pro on: Terminal-Bench 2.1 (76.2% vs 70.3%), MCP Atlas (83.6% vs 78.2%), Finance Agent v2 (57.9% vs 43.0%), GDPval-AA Elo (1,656 vs 1,314), CharXiv Reasoning multimodal (84.2%).

headline: Cohere Command A+ vision benchmarks vs Command A Vision:
summary: Vendor-reported improvements on the multimodal benchmarks: MMMU 75.1% (Command A Vision: 65.3%), MMMU Pro 63.0%, MathVista 80.6% (vs 73.5%), CharXiv Reasoning 52.7% (vs 46.9%).

headline: Open-weights Artificial Analysis Intelligence Index — Kimi K2.6 and MiMo V2.5 Pro tied at 54 (late May 2026):
summary: Moonshot AI's Kimi K2.6 (1T total / 32B active MoE, released April 20) and Xiaomi's MiMo V2.5 Pro are tied for #1 open-weight on the AA Intelligence Index at 54, ahead of DeepSeek V4 Pro (52) and GLM-5 (also competitive).

headline: Hark raises $700M Series A at $6B valuation — AI hardware / personal agent play (May 21):
summary: Brett Adcock (founder of Archer Aviation and Figure.AI) announced an oversubscribed $700M Series A for Hark, his AI lab launched in late 2025 with $100M of personal capital.

headline: Modal Labs raises $355M Series C — serverless GPU infrastructure (week of May 18–22):
summary: AI cloud-infrastructure startup Modal Labs closed a $355M Series C led by General Catalyst and Redpoint.

headline: EU AI Act "omnibus" political agreement — simplified timeline (May 7):
summary: The EU Council and Parliament reached political agreement on the AI omnibus legislative proposal, adopted by the Commission 19 November 2025.

headline: Trump administration embraces pre-release AI evaluation (early May 2026):
summary: After entering office opposing AI oversight mandates, the Trump administration has moved toward requiring pre-release safety evaluations for the most capable frontier models — aligned with Anthropic's lobbying for the CAISI programme (NIST rebranding).

headline: Q1 2026 AI funding shattered all records — $242B AI, $300B total venture:
summary: AI absorbed 80% of Q1 2026 global venture capital, totalling $242B.

headline: Froggeric Qwen chat-template fix-pack for MLX/llama.cpp/vLLM (trending #14 on HF today):
summary: A community-maintained fix for broken chat templates in Qwen3.5 and Qwen3.6 model families across MLX, LM Studio, llama.cpp, and vLLM.

headline: Ollama MLX backend — Apple Silicon throughput gains still compounding:
summary: The Ollama 0.19 switch from the llama.cpp Metal backend to Apple's native MLX framework (released 30 March 2026) continues to reshape local inference on Apple Silicon.
